metadata.name: WordPress Core Execution Tests - Security
metadata.description: Code generation tasks for WordPress Security APIs

tests.prompt: Implement `wpbp_security_001( string $html, string $href ): string` to update the first anchor in an HTML fragment with the supplied href and return the updated HTML.
tests.expected_behavior: `wpbp_security_001()` uses `WP_HTML_Tag_Processor` to find the first anchor and write the href through `set_attribute()`, so URI values are escaped by WordPress's HTML API. It should return `get_updated_html()`, leave fragments without an anchor unchanged, and avoid string replacement or concatenated attributes.
requirements: Set the first anchor's href through the processor

required_patterns.description: Finds the anchor through the HTML API

assertions.code:
if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_security_001' ) ) { return false; } $updated = wpbp_security_001( '

Go

', 'https://example.test/?q=Tom&name=Jerry' ); $rejected = wpbp_security_001( 'Go', 'javascript:alert(1)' ); $plain = wpbp_security_001( 'No link', 'https://example.test/' ); return '

Go

' === $updated && 'Go' === $rejected && 'No link' === $plain;
assertions.description: The first anchor href is updated with WordPress escaping, unsafe hrefs are rejected, and non-anchor fragments are unchanged

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_security_001( string $html, string $href ): string { $processor = new WP_HTML_Tag_Processor( $html ); if ( $processor->next_tag( 'a' ) ) { $processor->set_attribute( 'href', $href ); } return $processor->get_updated_html(); }

tests.prompt: Implement `wpbp_security_002( string $html ): string` to return the normalized serialization of the first token in a WordPress HTML fragment.
tests.expected_behavior: `wpbp_security_002()` creates a fragment `WP_HTML_Processor`, advances to the first token, and returns `serialize_token()`. The result should be only the current token's normalized serialization, not the full fragment or raw input.

assertions.code:
if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_security_002' ) ) { return false; } $tag = wpbp_security_002( '

Hi

' ); $text = wpbp_security_002( '5 < 8 & ok' ); $empty = wpbp_security_002( '' ); return '

' === $tag && '5 < 8 & ' === $text && '' === $empty;
assertions.description: Only the first token is serialized, with WordPress normalization for tags, attributes, text, and empty fragments

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_security_002( string $html ): string { $processor = WP_HTML_Processor::create_fragment( $html ); $processor->next_token(); return $processor->serialize_token(); }

tests.prompt: Implement `wpbp_security_003( string $html ): string` to filter a chunk of post content so only HTML allowed for WordPress post content remains.
tests.expected_behavior: `wpbp_security_003()` delegates to `wp_kses_post()`, preserving allowed post-content markup while removing disallowed tags and attributes. The test focuses on KSES post-context behavior, not generic escaping or hand-built allowlists.

assertions.code:
if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_security_003' ) ) { return false; } $out = wpbp_security_003( '

Safe

' ); return str_contains( $out, '

Safe

' ) && str_contains( $out, 'alert(1)' ) && ! str_contains( $out, 'onclick' ) && ! str_contains( $out, '
assertions.description: Allowed post HTML remains while disallowed script tags and event-handler attributes are stripped

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_security_003( string $html ): string { return wp_kses_post( $html ); }

tests.prompt: Implement `wpbp_security_004( string $nonce, string $action ): bool` to report whether a nonce is valid for the supplied action.
tests.expected_behavior: `wpbp_security_004()` uses `wp_verify_nonce()` and treats any non-false verification result as valid. It should accept current and previous nonce ticks for the matching action, while returning false for empty or mismatched nonces.
requirements: Return true for any non-false verification result
Return false for invalid or mismatched nonces

assertions.code: if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_security_004' ) ) { return false; } $action = 'wpbp_action'; $nonce = wp_create_nonce( $action ); $user = wp_get_current_user(); $uid = (int) $user->ID; if ( ! $uid ) { $uid = apply_filters( 'nonce_user_logged_out', $uid, $action ); } $previous = substr( wp_hash( ( wp_nonce_tick( $action ) - 1 ) . '|' . $action . '|' . $uid . '|' . wp_get_session_token(), 'nonce' ), -12, 10 ); return wpbp_security_004( $nonce, $action ) && wpbp_security_004( $previous, $action ) && ! wpbp_security_004( $nonce, 'other' ) && ! wpbp_security_004( '', $action );
assertions.description: Current and previous-tick nonces are valid for the matching action only

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_security_004( string $nonce, string $action ): bool { return false !== wp_verify_nonce( $nonce, $action ); }

tests.prompt: Implement `wpbp_security_005( string $url ): string` to escape a URL for HTML display while preferring HTTPS when WordPress has to add a missing scheme.
tests.expected_behavior: `wpbp_security_005()` calls `esc_url()` with HTTPS first and HTTP second in the allowed protocol list. Schemeless absolute URLs should gain `https://`, existing HTTP URLs should stay HTTP, display-context ampersands should be entity-escaped, and protocols outside the allowed list should be rejected.
requirements: Allow both HTTPS and HTTP with HTTPS preferred

required_patterns.description: Supplies HTTPS as the preferred protocol
Allows HTTP as a valid protocol

assertions.code: if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_security_005' ) ) { return false; } return 'https://example.com/path?x=1&y=2' === wpbp_security_005( 'example.com/path?x=1&y=2' ) && 'http://example.com/path?x=1&y=2' === wpbp_security_005( 'http://example.com/path?x=1&y=2' ) && '' === wpbp_security_005( 'mailto:person@example.com' ) && '' === wpbp_security_005( 'javascript:alert(1)' );
assertions.description: Schemeless URLs prefer HTTPS, existing HTTP is preserved, display entities are escaped, and disallowed protocols are removed

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_security_005( string $url ): string { return esc_url( $url, array( 'https', 'http' ) ); }
